what_it_does: This file is an online verification report that checks whether OpenWebUI's Channels inbound webhook endpoint and behavior are unchanged between v0.8.12 and the latest release, and it corrects a prior local finding about payload shape, identity handling, and documentation.
observations: The latest verified release is v0.9.6 (2026-06-01), not a vague '~0.9.x'.
The public webhook route is POST /api/v1/channels/webhooks/{webhook_id}/{token}, with no user authentication and a 32-byte secrets.token_urlsafe token.
The POST body accepts only {content}; custom name and avatar are set at webhook creation time and cannot vary per message.
The webhook path inserts the message and emits it live over socket.io but does not invoke the model-response handler, so @-mentioning a model from a webhook post does not summon a reply.
No rate-limiting decorator or global HTTP rate-limit middleware is present on the public webhook route.
The feature is documented on a dedicated admin docs page, contrary to the prior 'undocumented' claim.
The webhook was added in v0.7.0 and the load-bearing symbols are reported as byte-identical between v0.8.12 and v0.9.6.
v0.9.x added profile_image_url validation at webhook creation time and hardened channel access control on the model-mention path, neither of which changes the inbound webhook contract.
standouts: A public, unauthenticated-by-design inbound webhook with a per-webhook bearer token.
Fixed identity per webhook means each distinct agent persona needs its own webhook.
Webhook posts are push-only and do not trigger OpenWebUI's model pipeline.
The endpoint is documented, stable across the upgrade, and treated as a long-term integration point.
conventions: Uses an 'evidence discipline' with claims pinned to source-file-at-a-tag, CHANGELOG, PR, or docs URLs.
Labels findings as Confirmed or Inferred and distinguishes primary source from secondary/community precedent.
Puts a verdict up front, then sections for mechanism, version delta, documentation, stability, and precedent.
concerns: Release dates in 2026 are future-dated relative to the present, which may be placeholder or projection.
'Byte-identical' and 'byte-for-byte' claims are strong but the file does not show the diff or hash values.
The 'no rate limiting' conclusion is drawn from absence of decorators/middleware rather than an explicit negative test.
The file tracks :main, which drifts daily, so the 'identical to v0.9.6' claim is time-sensitive.
notes: A source-anchored cross-check confirming that OpenWebUI's channel inbound webhook is a stable, documented, no-auth push endpoint whose identity and payload semantics differ slightly from the earlier local note.
questions: What exact timestamp were the raw source files fetched, and was a diff or hash used to verify 'byte-identical' behavior?
Was a live smoke test actually executed, or is the curl example illustrative only?
Does the public webhook enforce any channel-level permissions beyond the global ENABLE_CHANNELS flag?
What was the actual state of the local install upgrade at the time of writing?
Are the 2026 release dates in the file real calendar dates or placeholders?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to verify online the local finding and the 0.8.12-to-latest delta, and the file does exactly that while correcting one prior claim.
apparent_themes: Evidence-first verification before relying on a Beta feature.
Treat webhook URLs as bearer secrets because they grant unauthenticated write access.
Design around behavioral asymmetries rather than assume symmetric request/response behavior.
Pin deploy images and verify-by-use after upgrade.
Distinguish documented contract from code-only behavior.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give the fabric project confidence that OpenWebUI's channel webhook can serve as a stable, public ingress for agent-generated messages after an upgrade, while correcting earlier misunderstandings about how identity and payload work.
novelty: It provides a detailed, version-anchored confirmation of a public webhook in a Beta feature, including a correction that the feature is documented and that the webhook identity is fixed at creation rather than passed per POST.
contribution: It contributes a source-cited, version-anchored confirmation of the webhook contract, corrected payload/identity semantics, and a set of stability caveats that a fused system can use when deciding whether to rely on this endpoint.
summary_for_embedding: An online verification report confirming OpenWebUI's Channels inbound webhook endpoint, its no-auth token model, content-only payload, fixed identity per webhook, push-only asymmetry, and stability from v0.8.12 to v0.9.6.
intention_for_embedding: To validate and correct the prior local finding about OpenWebUI's channel inbound webhook so the fabric can rely on it as a stable ingress after upgrading.
